About

Cybersecurity professional with 3.5+ years defending enterprise infrastructure. At IBM I served as Global Linux Security SME and Tier-3 Incident Response Lead. At Kyndryl I drove audit and compliance programs across critical systems. Currently finishing my M.S. in Cybersecurity at Pace University (GPA 3.92) and building open-source blue team tooling.

About

Threat hunter by day. Valorant victim by night.

Stuff I've Made
Brute Force Detector
The Bouncer
Detects SSH brute force attacks from auth logs in real time.
Built a bouncer that kicks out anyone who knocks too many times.
Network Anomaly Detector
The Weird-O-Meter
Flags unusual traffic patterns using baseline deviation analysis.
Taught my code to go "that's weird" so I don't have to.
Phishing URL Scanner
The Lie Detector
ML-assisted URL analysis to catch phishing domains before they land.
A spam detector with trust issues. Good trust issues.
SIEM Log Parser
The Translator
Normalizes multi-source logs into structured event records.
Made 10,000 lines of logs readable. Still not fun, but readable.
IOC Enrichment Tool
The Bad Guy Googler
Enriches indicators of compromise against threat intel APIs.
Googles bad guys automatically so I can spend more time on Valorant.
Linux Log Analyzer
The Grass Enabler
Scans Linux system logs for suspicious patterns and anomalies.
Taught a script to read logs so I can touch grass.
File Integrity Monitor
The File Bodyguard
Tracks unauthorized file modifications across watched directories.
Gets personally offended when someone touches files they shouldn't.
User Access Audit
The Zombie Hunter
SQL-driven access review detecting zombie accounts and privilege escalation.
Found the accounts nobody remembered existed. Classic zombie situation.
